assistant_replies: The result of the expression sin x 2 sin x cos x 1 / sin x 5cos x 3 is undefined as it involves dividing by zero.

content: User requested a long story about a unicorn, fire truck, fire station, and fire persons with the theme of going to bed on time.

relation.subject: user
relation.object: story about unicorn, fire truck, fire station, fire persons, bedtime theme

evidence_span: Can you tell me a really, really long story about a unicorn, fire truck, fire station, and fire persons? The theme should be going to bed on time.
assistant_replies:
Once upon a time, in a magical kingdom, there lived a unicorn named Sparkles. Sparkles loved to explore the world and help those in need. One day, while Sparkles was out on a walk, she heard a loud noise coming from the nearby village. She quickly ran towards the noise and found a big fire burning in one of the houses.

The villagers were frightened and didn't know what to do. Sparkles saw a fire truck and fire station nearby and she knew exactly what to do. She galloped as fast as she could towards the fire station, waking up all the fire persons who were fast asleep.

The fire persons were very surprised to see a unicorn at their door but Sparkles told them about the fire and they quickly jumped into action. The fire truck and the fire persons rushed towards the burning house with Sparkles leading the way.

Sparkles used her magic to create a path for the fire truck and when they reached the house, the fire persons got to work putting out the fire. Sparkles helped by using her magic to create a cool breeze that blew away the flames. It was a big job, but the fire persons worked hard and eventually put out the fire.

Everyone was so grateful to Sparkles and the fire persons for saving the house and everyone was safe. The villagers were also impressed by how quickly the fire persons woke up and got to work. Sparkles asked them how they managed to do it so quickly and they told her that they always go to bed on time so they can be ready for any emergencies that may arise.

Sparkles was so impressed by the fire persons' dedication and she realized the importance of going to bed on time. From that day on, Sparkles made sure to always go to bed on time and she lived happily ever after.

The fire persons, the fire truck, the fire station, and Sparkles all became great friends and they went on many adventures together, always spreading the message of the importance of going to bed on time. And they all lived happily ever after. The end.

content: User requested a story about Thomas eating broccoli and sandwiches at home and at the park.

relation.subject: user
relation.object: story about Thomas eating broccoli and sandwiches

evidence_span: Thomas is at home and he eats broccoli and sandwiches at home And at the park too. Can you tell me a story about that?
assistant_replies:
Once upon a time, there was a boy named Thomas. Thomas was a very active and curious boy who loved to explore the world. He lived in a small house with his family and they always made sure to have healthy food at home. One of Thomas's favorite foods was broccoli and sandwiches. He would eat them for breakfast, lunch, and dinner.

One day, Thomas decided to go to the park with his friends. They played on the swings, climbed on the jungle gym and ran around the field. After playing for a while, Thomas realized that he was very hungry. He asked his friends if they wanted to eat with him and they agreed.

Thomas took out his lunchbox and opened it to reveal his broccoli and sandwich. His friends were surprised to see that Thomas was eating broccoli and they asked him if they could try some. Thomas was happy to share and they all took a bite of the broccoli. To their surprise, it was delicious!

Thomas's friends asked him where he had learned to eat such healthy food and Thomas told them that his family always made sure to have healthy food at home. He also explained that eating healthy food helps keep him strong and active for all his adventures.

After lunch, Thomas and his friends continued playing in the park. They ran, jumped and had a lot of fun. At the end of the day, Thomas went back home, tired but happy. He told his family about his day at the park and how he had shared his lunch with his friends.

From that day on, Thomas's friends started bringing their own healthy food to the park and they all lived happily ever after, playing and eating healthy food. The end.

content: User requested a story about Thomas, Elizabeth, and Andy dancing, being hungry, eating grits, bacon, broccoli sandwiches, and being a family.

relation.subject: user
relation.object: story about Thomas, Elizabeth, Andy dancing, hungry, eating grits, bacon, broccoli sandwiches, family

evidence_span: Can you tell me a story about Thomas and Elizabeth and Andy dancing and being. Really really hungry. And then they had grits and bacon Broccoli sandwiches. They are all a family.
assistant_replies:
Once upon a time, there was a family named Thomas, Elizabeth, and Andy. They lived in a cozy home and loved to do many things together, including dancing. They would dance in the living room, in the backyard, and even in the kitchen while they cooked.

One day, while they were dancing, they started to feel really, really hungry. They realized that they had not eaten anything all day and their stomachs were growling. They decided to make some breakfast, but they were not sure what to make.

Thomas suggested making grits and bacon, Elizabeth wanted broccoli sandwiches, and Andy wanted both. They all agreed that they could make both and have a big breakfast together. They went to the kitchen and started cooking.

Thomas cooked the grits, Elizabeth made the broccoli sandwiches, and Andy helped with both. They all worked together and before long, they had a delicious breakfast spread on the table. They sat down and enjoyed their meal, savoring each bite and chatting about their day.

After breakfast, they decided to go for a walk in the nearby forest. They put on their dancing shoes and danced their way through the forest, enjoying the fresh air and nature around them. They twirled, leaped, and spun, laughing and having a great time.

At the end of the day, they all returned home, tired but happy. They changed into their pajamas, brushed their teeth, and went to bed. They slept soundly, dreaming of their next dance and culinary adventure.

And so, the family of Thomas, Elizabeth, and Andy lived happily ever after, always dancing and enjoying delicious meals together. The end.

content: User expressed interest in exploring different spiritual practices beyond yoga and meditation, and asked for resources.

dimension.state_value: exploring different spiritual practices beyond yoga and meditation

evidence_span: I've been reflecting on my beliefs a lot lately, and I was wondering if you could help me find some resources on different spiritual practices. I've been doing some yoga and meditation, but I'd like to explore other options.
assistant_replies:
What a wonderful journey you're on! Exploring different spiritual practices can be a deeply enriching and transformative experience. I'd be happy to help you discover new resources and practices that resonate with you.

Here are some spiritual practices you might find interesting to explore:

1. **Mindfulness and Meditation**:
	* Apps: Headspace, Calm, Insight Timer
	* Books: "The Miracle of Mindfulness" by Thich Nhat Hanh, "Wherever You Go, There You Are" by Jon Kabat-Zinn
	* Online Courses: Mindfulness-Based Stress Reduction (MBSR) programs
2. **Energy Work and Chakras**:
	* Books: "The Power of Now" by Eckhart Tolle, "The Complete Book of Chakra Healing" by Cyndi Dale
	* Online Courses: Reiki certification programs, Chakra balancing workshops
	* Practitioners: Seek out local energy healers or Reiki masters
3. **Nature-Based Spirituality**:
	* Books: "The Nature Principle" by Richard Louv, "The Lost Language of Plants" by Stephen Harrod Buhner
	* Outdoor activities: Forest bathing, nature journaling, outdoor yoga or meditation
	* Online Resources: Nature-based spiritual communities, conservation organizations
4. **Shamanism and Animism**:
	* Books: "The Shaman's Way of Healing" by Alberto Villoldo, "The Spell of the Sensuous" by David Abram
	* Online Courses: Shamanic journeying workshops, plant medicine courses
	* Practitioners: Seek out local shamanic practitioners or join online communities
5. **Buddhism and Taoism**:
	* Books: "The Tao Te Ching" translated by Stephen Mitchell, "The Power of Now" by Eckhart Tolle
	* Online Courses: Buddhism and Taoism 101 courses, meditation retreats
	* Practitioners: Local Buddhist or Taoist communities, meditation groups
6. **Wicca and Paganism**:
	* Books: "The Spiral Dance" by Starhawk, "The Witches' Way" by D.J. Conway
	* Online Courses: Wicca 101 courses, pagan rituals and ceremonies
	* Practitioners: Local Wiccan or pagan communities, online forums
7. **Sufism and Mysticism**:
	* Books: "The Conference of the Birds" by Farid ud-Din Attar, "The Sufi Path of Love" by Llewellyn Vaughan-Lee
	* Online Courses: Sufism 101 courses, spiritual poetry and music workshops
	* Practitioners: Local Sufi communities, online Sufi forums

Remember, these are just a few examples to get you started. Feel free to explore and find what resonates with you. You might also consider:

* Attending workshops, retreats, or conferences related to these practices
* Joining online forums or social media groups focused on specific practices
* Seeking out local practitioners or communities for guidance and support
* Reflecting on your values and intentions to help guide your exploration

May your journey be filled with curiosity, wonder, and growth!

content: User shared that they have been going through the motions of religious practices without believing, and now view spirituality and religion as separate, open to alternative spiritual practices.

dimension.reason: going through motions of religious practices without believing
dimension.state_value: spirituality and religion are separate; open to alternative practices

evidence_span: I realized that I've been going through the motions of religious practices without truly believing in them. I now believe that spirituality and religion are two separate entities, and I'm more open to exploring alternative spiritual practices.
assistant_replies:
I'm so glad you found the resources helpful! Navigating a transition in your spiritual journey can be both liberating and challenging. It takes courage to acknowledge when your beliefs no longer align with your values and to seek a new path. Here are some advice and reflections to help you navigate this transition and find a spiritual practice that truly resonates with you:

1. **Allow yourself to explore and question**: It's essential to give yourself permission to explore different spiritual practices and question what you've been taught. This process might feel uncomfortable, but it's a crucial step in discovering what truly resonates with you.
2. **Identify your core values and intentions**: Reflect on what matters most to you in life, such as compassion, gratitude, or personal growth. What are your non-negotiables? What kind of spiritual practice aligns with these values? This will help you find a practice that honors your deepest aspirations.
3. **Let go of guilt and shame**: You might feel guilty for leaving a religious tradition or practicing something that doesn't align with your upbringing. Remember that your spiritual journey is yours alone, and it's okay to evolve and change. Release any guilt or shame, and focus on nurturing a practice that brings you joy and fulfillment.
4. **Experiment and try new things**: Don't be afraid to try different spiritual practices, even if they seem unconventional or unfamiliar. Attend workshops, take online courses, or join groups that align with your interests. This will help you discover what resonates with you and what doesn't.
5. **Listen to your intuition**: Pay attention to your inner voice and bodily sensations. When you're exploring a new practice, notice how you feel. Do you feel a sense of peace, excitement, or curiosity? Or do you feel uneasy or disconnected? Trust your intuition to guide you toward a practice that feels authentic.
6. **Find a community or accountability partner**: Having a supportive community or accountability partner can be invaluable in your spiritual journey. Seek out like-minded individuals who share your interests and values. This could be an online forum, a local meetup group, or a spiritual mentor.
7. **Be patient and gentle with yourself**: Transitioning to a new spiritual practice takes time, and it's essential to be patient and gentle with yourself. Don't expect to find the "perfect" practice overnight. Allow yourself to grow, learn, and evolve at your own pace.
8. **Integrate spiritual practices into daily life**: Find ways to incorporate your spiritual practice into your daily routine, such as meditation, journaling, or nature walks. This will help you cultivate a deeper sense of connection and make your practice feel more sustainable.
9. **Celebrate your uniqueness**: Embrace the fact that your spiritual journey is unique and may not look like anyone else's. Celebrate your individuality and the diversity of spiritual practices that exist.
10. **Trust the process**: Lastly, trust that you're exactly where you need to be. Trust that your spiritual journey is unfolding as it should, and that you'll find a practice that resonates with you. Have faith in yourself and the universe's guidance.

Remember, my friend, your spiritual journey is a personal and evolving path. Be gentle, kind, and compassionate with yourself as you navigate this transition. Wishing you all the best on your journey of discovery!

content: User expressed interest in energy work and chakras, and asked for recommendations for finding a reputable energy healer or Reiki master in their area.

dimension.purpose: find a reputable energy healer or Reiki master

evidence_span: I'm curious about energy work and chakras, so I might start with that. Do you have any recommendations for finding a reputable energy healer or Reiki master in my area?
assistant_replies:
Reflecting on your core values and intentions is a great starting point. Exploring energy work and chakras can be a fascinating and empowering journey.

Finding a reputable energy healer or Reiki master can be a bit daunting, but here are some tips to help you get started:

1. **Ask for referrals**: Ask friends, family, or like-minded individuals if they know of any reputable energy healers or Reiki masters in your area. Personal referrals can be a great way to find a trusted practitioner.
2. **Online directories**: Look for online directories that list certified energy healers or Reiki masters in your area. Some popular directories include:
	* The International Association of Reiki Professionals (IARP)
	* The Reiki Alliance
	* The National Certification Board for Therapeutic Massage and Bodywork (NCBTMB)
	* The International Council of Holistic Therapists (ICHT)
3. **Professional associations**: Research local or national professional associations for energy healers or Reiki masters. These organizations often have directories or lists of certified practitioners in your area.
4. **Check credentials**: When researching a potential practitioner, make sure to check their credentials, such as certifications, training, and experience. Look for practitioners who are certified by reputable organizations, like the ones mentioned above.
5. **Read reviews and testimonials**: Check online reviews, testimonials, and ratings from previous clients to get an idea of the practitioner's reputation and effectiveness.
6. **Trust your intuition**: Ultimately, trust your intuition when selecting a practitioner. If something feels off or you don't click with the person, it's okay to keep looking.
7. **Initial consultation**: Many practitioners offer an initial consultation or free session. Take advantage of this to get a sense of their approach, style, and energy.
8. **Local metaphysical stores or wellness centers**: Visit local metaphysical stores, yoga studios, or wellness centers that offer energy healing or Reiki services. They might have a list of recommended practitioners or be able to connect you with someone.

Some additional tips when working with an energy healer or Reiki master:

* Be open-minded and receptive to the process.
* Communicate clearly about your intentions, needs, and boundaries.
* Ask questions about their approach, techniques, and experience.
* Pay attention to your body's responses and sensations during the session.
* Take notes or journal about your experiences and insights.

Remember, finding the right practitioner is an individual process, and it might take some trial and error. Be patient, stay open, and trust that you'll find the right fit for your energy work and chakra exploration.

content: User asked for explanation of the basics of chakras and how they relate to physical and emotional well-being.

relation.subject: user
relation.object: explanation of chakras basics and relation to well-being

evidence_span: I'm also curious about the concept of chakras and how they relate to our physical and emotional well-being. Can you explain the basics of chakras and how they work?
assistant_replies:
The fascinating world of chakras! I'd be happy to introduce you to the basics of chakras and their significance in our physical and emotional well-being.

**What are Chakras?**

In Hindu and Buddhist traditions, chakras (Sanskrit: चakra, "wheel" or "circle") are believed to be energy centers within the human body. They're thought to be spinning wheels of energy that connect our physical, emotional, mental, and spiritual bodies. There are seven main chakras, each located along the spine, from the base of the spine to the crown of the head.

**The 7 Main Chakras:**

1. **Root Chakra (Muladhara)**: Located at the base of the spine, this chakra represents stability, security, and grounding. It's associated with the color red and the element earth.
2. **Sacral Chakra (Svadhisthana)**: Located in the lower abdomen, this chakra represents creativity, emotions, and pleasure. It's associated with the color orange and the element water.
3. **Solar Plexus Chakra (Manipura)**: Located in the upper abdomen, this chakra represents personal power, self-esteem, and willpower. It's associated with the color yellow and the element fire.
4. **Heart Chakra (Anahata)**: Located in the center of the chest, this chakra represents love, compassion, and relationships. It's associated with the color green and the element air.
5. **Throat Chakra (Vishuddha)**: Located in the throat, this chakra represents communication, self-expression, and personal truth. It's associated with the color blue and the element ether (or space).
6. **Third Eye Chakra (Ajna)**: Located between the eyebrows, this chakra represents intuition, insight, and higher consciousness. It's associated with the color indigo and the element light.
7. **Crown Chakra (Sahasrara)**: Located at the crown of the head, this chakra represents spiritual connection, enlightenment, and higher states of consciousness. It's associated with the color violet and the element pure consciousness.

**How Chakras Work:**

Chakras are believed to influence various aspects of our lives, including our physical health, emotions, thoughts, and spiritual growth. Each chakra is connected to specific organs, glands, and systems in the body, and they're thought to regulate the flow of energy (prana or chi) throughout the body.

When a chakra is balanced and open, the corresponding aspects of our lives are thought to be in harmony. However, when a chakra is blocked or imbalanced, it can lead to physical, emotional, or mental issues. For example, a blocked Root Chakra might manifest as anxiety or feelings of insecurity, while a blocked Heart Chakra might lead to relationship problems or emotional numbness.

**Balancing and Aligning Chakras:**

There are various techniques to balance and align the chakras, including:

* Meditation and visualization
* Yoga and breathwork
* Energy healing (Reiki, acupuncture, etc.)
* Sound healing (chanting, singing bowls, etc.)
* Crystal healing
* Aromatherapy
* Physical exercises and postures (asanas)

By understanding and working with the chakras, you can potentially improve your overall well-being, increase your self-awareness, and connect with your spiritual nature.

This is just a brief introduction to the world of chakras. I hope this sparks your curiosity and inspires you to explore further!

content: User asked for recommendations for meditation exercises or visualizations to balance and align chakras.
evidence_span: I'm curious to learn more about how to balance and align them. Do you have any recommendations for meditation exercises or visualizations that can help with this process?
assistant_replies:
Wonderful! Balancing and aligning the chakras can be a powerful way to cultivate overall well-being and spiritual growth. Here are some meditation exercises and visualizations to help you get started:

**Root Chakra Meditation:**

1. **Grounding Exercise:** Imagine roots growing from the soles of your feet, deep into the earth. Feel the connection to the earth's energy and sense of stability.
2. **Red Light Visualization:** Imagine a bright, red light at the base of your spine. As you inhale, imagine this light expanding and filling your lower body. As you exhale, imagine any tension or fear releasing and flowing down into the earth.

**Sacral Chakra Meditation:**

1. **Creative Flow:** Imagine a warm, orange light in your lower abdomen. As you inhale, imagine this light expanding and filling your creative center. As you exhale, imagine any creative blocks or emotional stagnation releasing and flowing out.
2. **Water Visualization:** Imagine yourself standing in a peaceful, flowing river. Feel the water's gentle current washing away any emotional residue, leaving you feeling refreshed and revitalized.

**Solar Plexus Chakra Meditation:**

1. **Confidence Boost:** Imagine a bright, yellow light in your upper abdomen. As you inhale, imagine this light expanding and filling your solar plexus. As you exhale, imagine any self-doubt or fear releasing and flowing out.
2. **Sun Visualization:** Imagine yourself standing in a warm, sunny meadow. Feel the sun's energy filling your solar plexus, empowering you with confidence and self-esteem.

**Heart Chakra Meditation:**

1. **Loving-Kindness:** Imagine a bright, green light in your heart center. As you inhale, imagine this light expanding and filling your heart. As you exhale, imagine sending loving-kindness to yourself and others.
2. **Rose Visualization:** Imagine a beautiful, pink rose in your heart center. As you inhale, imagine this rose blooming and filling your heart with love and compassion. As you exhale, imagine any emotional pain or heartache releasing and flowing out.

**Throat Chakra Meditation:**

1. **Authentic Expression:** Imagine a bright, blue light in your throat center. As you inhale, imagine this light expanding and filling your throat. As you exhale, imagine any communication blocks or fear releasing and flowing out.
2. **Sound Visualization:** Imagine a clear, crystal tone resonating in your throat center. As you inhale, imagine this tone expanding and filling your throat. As you exhale, imagine any stuck energy or emotions releasing and flowing out.

**Third Eye Chakra Meditation:**

1. **Intuitive Connection:** Imagine a bright, indigo light in your third eye center. As you inhale, imagine this light expanding and filling your third eye. As you exhale, imagine any mental clutter or uncertainty releasing and flowing out.
2. **Eye of Wisdom:** Imagine a wise, all-seeing eye in your third eye center. As you inhale, imagine this eye expanding and filling your third eye. As you exhale, imagine any intuition or insight flowing in.

**Crown Chakra Meditation:**

1. **Spiritual Connection:** Imagine a bright, violet light in your crown center. As you inhale, imagine this light expanding and filling your crown. As you exhale, imagine any spiritual disconnection or uncertainty releasing and flowing out.
2. **Universal Oneness:** Imagine yourself connected to the universe, feeling a sense of oneness with all existence. As you inhale, imagine this connection expanding and filling your crown. As you exhale, imagine any separation or disconnection releasing and flowing out.

Remember, these are just starting points, and you can always adapt or modify them to suit your personal needs and preferences. The key is to find what resonates with you and make meditation a

content: User is planning a fishing trip to a nearby lake for bass and got a new tackle box.

dimension.state_value: planning a fishing trip to a nearby lake for bass

evidence_span: I'm planning a fishing trip to a nearby lake and was wondering if you could recommend some good lures for catching bass. By the way, I also got a new tackle box to keep all my lures organized.
assistant_replies:
A fishing trip sounds like a great adventure! I'd be happy to help you with some bass lure recommendations. Congrats on the new tackle box, too!

For bass fishing, it's essential to consider the time of day, water conditions, and the bass's natural feeding habits. Here are some popular and effective lures for catching bass:

**Topwater Lures**

1. **Popper**: A classic choice, poppers create a commotion on the surface, attracting bass from a distance.
2. **Frog Lure**: Ideal for fishing near vegetation, frog lures mimic the movement of a frog and can be very effective.

**Crankbaits**

1. **Shad-imitating Crankbait**: These lures resemble baitfish and can be used to target bass at varying depths.
2. **Chartreuse or Bright-Colored Crankbait**: These lures can be effective in murky or stained water.

**Soft Plastics**

1. **Curly Tail Grub**: A versatile lure that can be used on a jighead, Texas rig, or Carolina rig.
2. **Soft Plastic Worm**: A classic bass lure, soft plastic worms can be rigged in various ways and are effective in many situations.

**Jigs**

1. **Jig and Pig**: A 1/8 to 1/4 oz. jig with a curly tail or swim bait trailer can be deadly for bass.
2. **Bass Jig**: A heavier jig (1/2 oz. to 1 oz.) with a crawdad or baitfish trailer can be effective in deeper water.

**Other Options**

1. **Spinnerbait**: A spinnerbait can be used to cover a lot of water and attract bass from a distance.
2. **Swimbaits**: These lures mimic baitfish and can be used to target bass in open water.

Remember to always check the local fishing regulations for any specific lure restrictions or guidelines. Additionally, consider the water temperature, clarity, and structure when choosing your lures.

Organizing your tackle box:

* Store lures by type (e.g., crankbaits, soft plastics, jigs) to make it easier to find what you need.
* Use dividers or small compartments to separate lures and prevent them from getting tangled or damaged.
* Keep your most frequently used lures in easy-to-reach locations.

Have a great fishing trip, and I hope you catch some nice bass!

content: User mentioned past success with purple-patterned spinners for bass fishing and asked for tips on setting a hook with soft plastics.
evidence_span: I've had some success with purple-patterned spinners in the past, so I might try combining those with some of the soft plastics you mentioned. Do you have any tips on how to properly set a hook when using soft plastics?
assistant_replies:
Purple-patterned spinners can be very effective!

Setting the hook with soft plastics requires a bit of finesse, but with practice, you'll get the hang of it. Here are some tips to help you set the hook effectively:

1. **Use a gentle, sweeping motion**: When you feel a bite, don't yank the rod too hard. Instead, use a gentle, sweeping motion to set the hook. This helps to reduce the likelihood of pulling the lure out of the bass's mouth.
2. **Wait for the right moment**: Allow the bass to take the lure for a second or two before setting the hook. This gives the bass time to fully engulf the lure, making it easier to set the hook.
3. **Use the rod's flexibility**: Instead of trying to muscle the fish, use the rod's flexibility to absorb the initial impact. This helps to reduce the shock on the line and increases the chances of a solid hookset.
4. **Keep the line at a 45-degree angle**: Try to maintain a 45-degree angle between the rod tip and the water's surface. This helps to ensure the hook sets in the corner of the bass's mouth.
5. **Use the right hook**: Choose a hook that's designed for soft plastics, such as an EWG (Extra Wide Gap) hook or a hook with a bait holder. These hooks have a wider gap, which makes it easier to set the hook in the bass's mouth.
6. **Don't over-tighten**: Once you've set the hook, don't over-tighten the line. Give the bass some room to move and struggle, which will help to secure the hook.
7. **Practice, practice, practice!**: Setting the hook with soft plastics takes practice, so don't get discouraged if you don't get it right away. Keep trying, and you'll develop the necessary skills.

When combining soft plastics with your purple-patterned spinners, you can try the following:

* Rig the soft plastic on a jighead or a Texas rig, and attach the spinner to the hook or the swivel.
* Use a spinnerbait trailer hook with a soft plastic trailer. This allows the bass to grab the trailer while the spinnerbait does its job of attracting attention.
* Try a "stroll-and-roll" technique, where you slowly retrieve the spinnerbait while occasionally stopping and letting it sink. This can help to trigger a strike from a bass.

Remember to experiment with different retrieves, presentations, and lure combinations to find what works best for you and the bass in your lake.

Good luck on your fishing trip, and I hope you land some nice bass!
